notes: Feature definitions adapted from StoryScope (Russell et al. 2026, arXiv:2604.03136), Tables 13-15.
ai_lean / human_lean are directional priors from a corpus of published fiction vs one-shot model generations.
They are NOT thresholds and NOT evidence of authorship. mlab never computes an authorship score.
A value matching ai_lean is only worth attention when it is unchosen, saturated across sections, or fights the contract.

features.label: Thematic explicitness
features.question: Does the narration state the theme or moral outright?

features.label: Narrator thematic commentary
features.question: Does the narrator explicitly comment on themes beyond the characters' perspectives?

features.label: Dialogue as philosophical debate
features.question: Does dialogue serve mainly to debate ideas or deliver the theme?

features.label: Moral stance toward protagonist
features.question: Does the narrative frame the protagonist's choices as morally clear or ambiguous?

features.label: Dominant emotional expression
features.question: How are characters' emotions most commonly conveyed?

features.label: Setting as psychological mirror
features.question: Does the physical environment mirror characters' inner states?

features.label: Sensory density
features.question: How dense is sensory description across the section?

features.label: Smell-based imagery present
features.question: Does the section lean on smell-based imagery?

features.label: Character introduction mode
features.question: How are new characters primarily introduced?

features.label: Causal chain continuity
features.question: How continuous is the main causal chain through the section?

features.label: Subplot presence and relation
features.question: Are there subplots, and how do they relate to the central thread?

features.label: Mode of resolution
features.question: Is the section's main tension resolved externally, internally, or left open?

features.label: Agency in resolution
features.question: Is the resolution driven by the protagonist's choices or external events?

features.label: Scene-level turn present
features.question: Does at least one event genuinely turn the situation?

features.label: Loose ends left open
features.question: Does the section leave consequences or threads deliberately open?

features.label: Temporal order
features.question: Does the narrative move strictly forward in time?

features.label: Time devices in play
features.question: How many distinct time devices (flashback, flash-forward, jump, recontextualization) does the section use?

features.label: Revelation recontextualizes earlier material
features.question: Does a reveal force reinterpretation of earlier scenes?

features.label: Direct reader address
features.question: Does the narration directly address the reader?

features.label: Location variety
features.question: How many distinct locations does the section inhabit?

features.label: Dialogue-to-narration proportion
features.question: What share of the section is direct dialogue?

features.label: Intertextual reference explicitness
features.question: Are references to other works, brands, or places vague echoes or named?
